lessons.title: Directory Navigation
lessons.content_md:
## Directory Navigation

In Linux, the file system starts from a single root directory `/`.

### Key Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `pwd` | Print current directory |
| `ls` | List directory contents |
| `ls -la` | List all files with details |
| `cd /path` | Change directory |
| `cd ..` | Go up one directory |
| `cd ~` | Go to home directory |

### Try it

```bash
pwd
ls /
cd /etc
pwd
```

tasks.description: Navigate to the /etc directory and confirm with pwd.
List all files in /etc including hidden files.

lessons.title: Files and Directories
lessons.content_md:
## Files and Directories

### Creating Files & Directories

```bash
mkdir mydir          # Create directory
touch myfile.txt     # Create empty file
echo 'text' > file   # Create file with content
```

### Viewing Files

```bash
cat file.txt         # Print file content
less file.txt        # Paginate through file
head -5 file.txt     # First 5 lines
tail -5 file.txt     # Last 5 lines
```

tasks.description: Create a directory called 'mylab' in your home directory.
Create a file called 'notes.txt' inside mylab with the text 'B-HackMe'.

lessons.title: File Operations
lessons.content_md:
## File Operations

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `cp src dest` | Copy file |
| `mv src dest` | Move / rename file |
| `rm file` | Delete file |
| `rm -rf dir` | Delete directory recursively |

### Example

```bash
cp notes.txt notes_backup.txt
mv notes_backup.txt archive.txt
rm archive.txt
```
